implications: Failure rate increases from 18.4% (turn_1) to 41.1% (turn_4+), indicating that the system struggles with longer dialogues. Context-aware query rewriting or conversation memory should be explored.

241 queries fail with rewrite but succeed without — the rewrite changes the user's intent. Rewrite models need to be more conservative and context-aware, or a fallback mechanism should route ambiguous rewrites to the original query.

Ranking errors (2664) outnumber retrieval errors (1364). The candidate pool contains the relevant documents but the ranking model fails to surface them. Investing in stronger rerankers (e.g., cross-encoder fine-tuning) is the priority.
Rerankers can only help when recall is sufficient: 2664 ranking errors are potentially rescuable, while 1364 retrieval errors are fundamentally out of reach for any reranker.
